data.title: BYDFi Joins Blockchain Forum 2026 in Moscow as Sponsor, Engaging the Web3 and AI Ecosystem
data.description: Seychelles-based global cryptocurrency exchange, BYDFi, has announced its sponsorship of the Blockchain Forum 2026. The event is scheduled to occur from April 14 to 15 at Crocus Expo, Pavilion 2 in Moscow. Blockchain Forum 2026 is the 16th edition of the event organized by the same team responsible for Blockchain Life. By participating as a sponsor, BYDFi aims to enhance its presence and engagement within the cryptocurrency and blockchain community attending the forum.

data.title: SurgePays (SURG) Stock Falls 30% Overnight: What's Going On?
data.description: SurgePays, Inc. (NASDAQ: SURG) experienced a drop in after-hours trading following the release of its full-year 2025 financial results. The company highlighted advancements in cost efficiency and a transition to a more capital-efficient growth strategy. In terms of financial performance, SurgePays reported annual revenue of around $57 million, a decrease from $60.9 million in 2024 due to the completion of the Affordable Connectivity Program in mid-2024. The gross loss showed improvement at $10.6 million compared to $14.3 million in the previous year, while operating loss decreased to $30.7 million from $41.8 million. General and administrative expenses also saw a decline to approximately $20.1 million from $27.5 million, showcasing reduced operating costs. CEO Brian Cox mentioned that the company displayed scalability in 2025, with revenue rising sequentially in the first three quarters before declining.

data.title: Sivers Semiconductors Collaborates With Jabil on Energy Efficient 1.6T Pluggable Optical Transceiver Module
data.description: In KISTA, Sweden, on April 15, 2026, Sivers Semiconductors AB (STO: SIVE), a prominent company in photonics and wireless technologies, revealed a partnership with Jabil. This collaboration aims to enhance performance in AI data centers by providing significantly faster speeds while consuming 2.5 times less energy.

data.title: FPT Advances Chugoku Electric Power Group’s Digital Transformation Through Microsoft 365 Deployment
data.description: In Hanoi, Vietnam, FPT has recently entered a significant agreement to supply Microsoft 365 (M365) licenses to Chugoku Electric Power Group, a major player in Japan's energy sector. The power group is recognized as one of Japan's top ten regional power utilities and is specifically ranked sixth in terms of electricity sales. This partnership highlights FPT's dominant position in Japan's technology industry and its crucial involvement in fostering innovation and promoting the digital evolution of the Hiroshima-based Chugoku Electric Power Group. This deal further strengthens the existing partnership between FPT and Chugoku Ele, showcasing a key step in enhancing technological capabilities within the energy sector.

data.title: Gimme Credit Launches European High Yield Credit Research Service Expanding Its Global Coverage of Widely Held, Liquid Corporate Bonds
data.description: On April 15, 2026, Gimme Credit, a top independent provider of corporate bond analysis and suggestions, revealed the introduction of its European High Yield Research Service. This move signifies the company's dedication to offering timely and comprehensive coverage globally.

data.title: RIMAN's Parent Company Announces Sponsored Research Agreement to Advance Araliadiol Using Organ-on-a-Chip Technology
data.description: On April 15, 2026, ASK Company, the owner of RIMAN, has partnered with the Mitragotri Laboratory at Harvard John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences for sponsored research. The agreement aims to validate the functionality and effectiveness of their products.

data.title: OSE Immunotherapeutics to Present a Novel, First-in-Class, FPR2 Agonist Monoclonal Antibody in the Resolution of Chronic Inflammation  at the AAI 2026 Meeting
data.description: OSE Immunotherapeutics will showcase their innovative FPR2 agonist monoclonal antibody, a first-of-its-kind, for treating chronic inflammation at the AAI 2026 conference. This antibody targets FPR2, a key receptor involved in regulating inflammation. The presentation aims to highlight the antibody's potential in resolving chronic inflammation. This development represents a significant advancement in immunotherapy research and holds promise for improving patient outcomes.
